video:video.video:title: South Korea coach and team booed by angry fans at airport after World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Former South Korea head coach Hong Myung-bo and nine of the 26 players in the South Korea squad landed at Incheon International Airport near Seoul under heavy security.

video:video.video:title: Singing and selfies to launch new 'Moana'
video:video.video:description: Dwayne Johnson performs "You're Welcome," as he joins "Moana" stars old and new, surprising fans at a London event celebrating the upcoming live action remake.

video:video.video:title: Morocco fans gather in Monterrey before knockout clash with the Netherlands
video:video.video:description: Morocco supporters gathered at Monterrey's Macroplaza on Sunday, creating a festive atmosphere ahead of their team's Round of 32 World Cup match against the Netherlands.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 30th: U.S. and Iran appear to have paused strikes; Concern grows for people left homeless by Venezuela earthquakes; Supreme Court rules mailed ballots that arrived late can be counted, with proper postmark; Paraguay beats Germany in World Cup upset.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on political party spending, backing Republican appeal
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has erased limits on how much political parties can spend in coordination with candidates for Congress and president, striking down a federal election law that's more than 50 years old.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court upholds laws barring transgender girls and women from competing on school sports teams
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has upheld state laws barring transgender girls and women from competing on high school and college sports teams in another setback for transgender rights.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: A heat dome will engulf World Cup games in the eastern US this week. Here's what to expect
video:video.video:description: The World Cup games are heating up this week with knockout rounds, and a dangerous heat dome that will engulf the central and eastern U.S.

video:video.video:title: Dogs splash through pool as Mississippi animal shelter weathers extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Amid soaring temperatures, dogs at a Jackson, Mississippi animal shelter with no air conditioning cooled off in a splash pool Tuesday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: UN's World Food Programme distributes food to quake victims in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) increased its operations in Venezuela, distributing food assistance and establishing temporary feeding centres to support affected communities.

video:video.video:title: AI has a huge environmental footprint
video:video.video:description: As the world tries to curb human-caused climate change and not run dry of water, every online query is increasing our environmental footprint and exacerbating the problem.

video:video.video:title: How to normalize money conversations with your kids
video:video.video:description: Money talk isn't just for adults anymore. In a world of hidden subscription fees and rising student loan debt, parents are shifting from shielding kids from money to teaching them how to master it.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists he doesn't manage his investments after $1.2B crypto earnings disclosure
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Wednesday he is not involved in managing his finances, insisting outside funds handle his investments.

video:video.video:title: World Cup buzz has the potential to mint more soccer fans among kids — some just toddlers now
video:video.video:description: Across the country, experts are watching to see whether the World Cup will give a boost to youth soccer and mint fans for decades to come.

video:video.video:title: Funerals of children killed in roof collapse at tutoring centre under construction in Lahore
video:video.video:description: Mourners gathered Wednesday in Pakistan’s eastern city of Lahore to bury 14 schoolchildren who were killed when the roof of a tutoring center collapsed on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Spectacular orange lava flows from Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano
video:video.video:description: An eruption of Mount Etna, on the Italian island of Sicily, produced a spectacular lava show that turned part of Europe's largest active volcano orange.

video:video.video:title: Marine heat wave off California coast leaves seabirds starving or dead
video:video.video:description: Many seabirds are starving to death as a marine heat wave lingers off California and fish seek deeper, cooler waters, scientists say.

video:video.video:title: From a little house to a bigger stage, Motown Museum readies its $75M expansion
video:video.video:description: A $75 million expansion of the Motown Museum in Detroit is entering its final phases ahead of a grand opening next year. (AP video: Mike Householder and Corey Williams)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Lawyers for E. Jean Carroll demand $5 million from Trump; Supreme Court rejects Trump’s birthright citizenship limits; Democratic Socialist wins Colorado U.S.

video:video.video:title: Roof collapse at tutoring centre under construction in Lahore kills at least 14 children
video:video.video:description: A roof collapse at a tutoring center in Pakistan’s Kahna Town of Lahore District killed at least 14 schoolchildren on Tuesday, according to the police and rescue officials.

video:video.video:title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, rejecting Trump’s proposed limits
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has rejected President Donald Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship that would have denied citizenship to children born to mothers who are in the country illegally or temporarily.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Trump administration silent on Iranian school strike four months later
video:video.video:description: It was the deadliest reported strike in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, and most of the victims were children.

video:video.video:title: Anti-immigration protesters march in South Africa
video:video.video:description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in parts of South Africa to rally against illegal immigration in the biggest migration-related protests since the wave of anti-migrant violence in 2008.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations erupt at San Diego watch party as Mexico tops Ecuador, 2-0
video:video.video:description: The crowd at a World Cup watch party in San Diego, California erupted in raucous celebrations on Tuesday night as Mexico topped Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup round of 32 soccer match.

video:video.video:title: Hospital in France gears up for the next heat wave armed with lessons learnt from previous ones
video:video.video:description: At a Paris-region hospital, emergency medics needed it to plunge patients into cold-water baths to speedily bring down their temperatures so they wouldn't join the growing tally of dead from a record-smashing heat wave.

video:video.video:title: Italy displays paintings from an ancient Etruscan tomb, its latest cultural acquisition
video:video.video:description: Italy on Tuesday put on display one of the best known examples of Etruscan painting, from a tomb that has become the latest acquisition in the Culture Ministry’s buying spree of big-ticket pieces of the country’s cultural heritage.

video:video.video:title: Vic Mensa on his short film 'HALFRICAN'
video:video.video:description: Vic Mensa says his short film "HALFRICAN" was "a labor of community."

video:video.video:title: People experiencing homelessness seek respite from the heat at a cooling center in Georgia
video:video.video:description: People without access to housing are getting a break from the heat at a homeless shelter in Georgia.

video:video.video:title: Leon Thomas: 'R&B is home for me'
video:video.video:description: Leon Thomas explains the impact R&B music has had on his life and artistry. He was honored with the ASCAP Vanguard Award.

video:video.video:title: Bone found in a drawer turns out to be a dinosaur from Antarctica
video:video.video:description: A bone found in a drawer has turned out to be a dinosaur from Antarctica. Researchers compared the fossil to other more complete dinosaur remains and have described it as belonging to the titanosaur group.
video:video.video:tag: Box Butte County Natural History Museum

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 30: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship; Aid groups warn Venezuela’s healthcare system is near its limit; U.S. envoys arrive in Qatar for Iran war mediation; Sweden and France face-off at World Cup win-or-go-home match.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Inde Navarette says working on 'Obsession' unlocked a newfound confidence within herself
video:video.video:description: Inde Navarrette says working on "Obsession" gave her a newfound confidence in herself. Navarrette's career has skyrocketed her to global fame and established her as a fan-favorite scream queen.

video:video.video:title: Jordan Chiles: 'Women can do anything'
video:video.video:description: Jordan Chiles talks about the importance of support for women's sports. She was nominated for sportswoman of the year at the BET Awards.

video:video.video:title: AP/NORC poll: Who flies the US flag, who won’t and what it signals about America
video:video.video:description: New AP/NORC poll indicates Americans’ views of the flag are divided by politics, age and race (AP video: Mike Householder

video:video.video:title: June Diane Raphael talks about working with James Van Der Beek in 'Elle'
video:video.video:description: James Van Der Beek, who died in February after a bowel cancer diagnosis, had a recurring role in Season 1 of the Prime Video series. (June 29)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Trump flies on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar; Data shows Trump made $1.2 billion from crypto; Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iran says; Traditionalist Catholics defy Pope Leo with bishop consecration.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce to celebrate wedding at Madison Square Garden
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will celebrate their wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday night, according to a law enforcement official briefed on the security plans.

video:video.video:title: Egg producers will pay $3.3M and donate 53 million eggs to settle price fixing claims
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states have reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers over allegations that the companies illegally colluded in a multiyear scheme to raises prices.

video:video.video:title: 3 people die of asphyxiation during Mexico City celebrations after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum expressed her condolences on Wednesday after three people died of asphyxiation during massive celebrations in downtown Mexico City after the national team’s victory over Ecuador in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: From Chocolate Avenue to the World Cup, how Hershey, Pennsylvania, shaped Christian Pulisic
video:video.video:description: Standout U.S. national soccer team player Christian Pulisic hails from Hershey, Pennsylvania. Known as the “Sweetest Place on Earth” thanks to its chocolate-drenched origins.

video:video.video:title: Children found in ‘deplorable’ Ohio home were part of same family
video:video.video:description: Officials say 16 children found living in “deplorable” conditions inside an Ohio home are part of the same family.

video:video.video:title: Onlookers react to banner atop Empire State Building
video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:title: Maher jokes White House called his Mark Twain Prize "fake news"
video:video.video:description: Before accepting the Mark Twain Prize, Bill Maher poked fun at the White House, which had labeled news of his selection for the honor as "fake news" when it was announced.

video:video.video:title: Trump takes first flight on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One Wednesday before its initial voyage to North Dakota, saying the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh emphasizes political independence, signals focus on inflation
video:video.video:description: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that the central bank would remain independent and seek to bring down inflation, likely foreclosing the rate cuts President Donald Trump has sought.

video:video.video:title: 2 people arrested after hanging banner about 'the power of love' on Empire State Building antenna
video:video.video:description: Two people who got to the top of the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” have been arrested.

video:video.video:title: Panda birthdays at Hong Kong Zoo
video:video.video:description: A group of pandas in Hong Kong are celebrating their birthdays at the amusement park where they live.

video:video.video:title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, a first for a Chinese American in the state's history
video:video.video:description: Martial arts icon Bruce Lee, who was born in San Francisco, will become the first Chinese American in California history with an annual namesake day.

video:video.video:title: Sightings of humpback whales surge in Rio de Janeiro, fueling demand for whale-watching trips
video:video.video:description: Sightings of humpback whales off Rio de Janeiro’s coast are surging as they recover from decimation due to commercial whaling, prompting an acceleration in the demand for whale-watching excursions to spot the huge marine creatures during their annual migration.

video:video.video:title: Attorney says autopsy of 1-year-old shot by Mississippi police conflicts with officers' account
video:video.video:description: A Mississippi family whose 1-year-old child was killed when officers fired on a car are challenging police claims about the shooting.

video:video.video:title: New York bar owner calls street closures and added security for Swift-Kelce wedding ‘ridiculous’
video:video.video:description: Co-owner of O'Briens Bar and Grill located across from Madison Square Garden questioned if the couple can afford the permits to close the streets and a “big lavish ceremony” then why not also buy out the nearby businesses so they are not adversely affected.

video:video.video:title: Will the World Cup spark soccer interest in kids?
video:video.video:description: The buzz around the FIFA World Cup has the potential to mint more soccer fans among kids — some just toddlers now.

video:video.video:title: New England ice supplier describes joy and challenges of higher demand sparked by extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Extreme heat baking large parts of the U.S. generates extra business – and grueling working conditions – for New England ice vendor ahead of Fourth of July festivities in a hub of country's independence (AP Video and Production: Rodrique Ngowi.)

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows officers arresting a couple after climbing the Empire State Building
video:video.video:description: Bodycam footage shows officers arresting two people after scaling the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurling a banner about “the power of love.”

video:video.video:title: Powerful storm strikes Romania ’s capital, killing one person and causing flooding
video:video.video:description: A powerful storm struck Romania ’s capital overnight into Wednesday, killing one person and causing flooding in 20 counties. (AP Video by Nick Dumitrache)

video:video.video:title: Three-year-old boy rescued from Venezuela quake rubble after being trapped for 6 days
video:video.video:description: The sole survivor rescued in Venezuela by Tuesday afternoon was a toddler who had been trapped for six days under a collapsed building, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said.

video:video.video:title: The world's largest digital camera begins decade-long survey of the universe
video:video.video:description: The largest digital camera ever built is starting to capture images of unseen corners of the universe. The Vera C.

video:video.video:title: Four adults charged after 16 children found in 'deplorable' conditions in southern Ohio home
video:video.video:description: The children are part of the same family, officials said. The four people charged appeared in court Wednesday where a judge entered not guilty pleas on their behalf.

video:video.video:title: How does the UK get a new Prime Minister without a general election?
video:video.video:description: Recently elected UK lawmaker Andy Burnham has been referred to as "likely Britain's next Prime Minister", following the resignation of current Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeats longtime US House incumbent in Colorado
video:video.video:description: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros beat U.S. Rep. Diana DeGette in a Colorado House primary Tuesday, a stunning victory for the first-time candidate against a nearly 30-year incumbent and another win for progressive challengers across the country.

video:video.video:title: Fire rips through an Ohio motel, killing 3 who were trapped in a room
video:video.video:description: Wooster Township Fire Chief said firefighters made several attempts to reach the people who were trapped.

video:video.video:title: Man escapes apartment fire through neighbor's window in Antwerp
video:video.video:description: Dramatic footage showed a man escaping from a fire at an apartment block on the outskirts of the Belgian city of Antwerp, climbing from a balcony to a nearby window.

video:video.video:title: Police at scene after explosion in Monaco, witness recounts moments after blast
video:video.video:description: Police were at the scene in Monaco on Tuesday after an explosive went off in an apartment building entrance, wounding three people, reportedly including a Ukrainian tycoon with ties to Russia.

video:video.video:title: 'Kiki's Delivery Service' author says book was inspired by daughter's witch drawing
video:video.video:description: Eiko Kadono, the author of the novel behind Hayao Miyazaki's film "Kiki's Delivery Service," discusses her love of writing and how the book came to be. (July 1)

video:video.video:title: Three injured in Russian strike on Kramatorsk as Ukraine says it downs 130 drones
video:video.video:description: Three people were injured in a Russian airstrike on a residential area of Kramatorsk in Ukraine's Donetsk region.

video:video.video:title: Waterspout hits Lake Constance in Germany
video:video.video:description: A waterspout formed over a lake in Germany in a rare weather event that caused a stir in the area. The phenomenon was seen forming over Lake Constance near Lindau on Wednesday, German news agency DPA reported.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis
video:video.video:description: The lines are growing at Russian gas stations -- and so is the frustration and anxiety as months of Ukrainian attacks have set oil refineries ablaze and choked supplies for motorists across the vast country.

video:video.video:title: Lexi and Reese share Stella McCartney dress
video:video.video:description: Lexi Minetree says "it's crazy" she got to wear Reese Witherspoon's Stella McCartney dress from the 2014 Met Gala at the premiere of her TV show "Elle" in London

video:video.video:title: Renewable energy and coal coexist in Inner Mongolia's electricity plan
video:video.video:description: China's Inner Mongolia is expanding both renewable energy and coal use. Despite the growth of solar and wind farms, coal remains essential.

video:video.video:title: Olivia Wilde reacts to England win
video:video.video:description: Olivia Wilde reacts to England win

video:video.video:title: Retrofitted Qatari jet takes flight as Air Force One for Trump's trip to North Dakota
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday took his maiden voyage on a new Air Force One — a retrofitted Boeing 747-800 worth $400 million gifted by Qatar.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack on Ukraine shakes Kyiv for hours
video:video.video:description: Russian attack on Ukraine shakes Kyiv for hours

video:video.video:title: What we know about Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will celebrate their wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday night, according to a law enforcement official briefed on the security plans.

video:video.video:title: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza
video:video.video:description: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza

video:video.video:title: Panini sticker swapping continues during 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: More than 50 years since their creation, Panini World Cup stickers continue to bring soccer fans together around the world.

video:video.video:title: 2 people arrested after hanging a banner about 'the power of love' on Empire State Building antenna
video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building's antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military close entrances to West Bank town, disrupting residents' lives
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the town of Sinjil in the Israeli-occupied West Bank woke up Wednesday to find checkpoints to the village closed by Israeli troops, upending plans and disrupting daily lives and schedules.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister welcomes Japanese Prime Minister to New Delhi
video:video.video:description: Japan's leader was in India to meet with the country's prime minister. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi was greeted in New Delhi by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians across Gaza Strip reflect on pre-war lives, marking 1,000 days of war
video:video.video:description: One thousand days have passed since the Hamas-led attack on Israel sparked the war in Gaza. Other conflicts have emerged in the region, and fragile ceasefires show scars of persistent attacks.

video:video.video:title: Global fans embrace Lucha Libre in Mexico City during World Cup
video:video.video:description: Travelers from all over the world have succumbed to the magic of Mexico’s "Lucha Libre," which, alongside soccer, is a national passion. (AP video shot by Marko Alvarez)

video:video.video:title: Trump filing shows he took in about $1.2 billion from crypto businesses last year
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump took in nearly $1.2 billion dollars from his crypto businesses last year, a federal filing shows. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Defying Pope Leo XIV and risking schism, traditionalists go ahead with Latin Mass consecrations
video:video.video:description: The Society of St. Pius X, which opposes modernizing reforms in the Catholic Church, went ahead with a ritual-filled ceremony at its seminary in Econe, Switzerland, despite a last-ditch appeal by Leo to call it off.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers pull the injured from rubble in Kyiv after Russian attack
video:video.video:description: Russian missiles and drones kill at least 8 people in Kyiv, causing damage across Ukrainian capital
